questions.q: What does the word 'Swadeshi' broadly mean?
questions.explanation: 'Swadeshi' means 'of one's own country', referring to the use of indigenous goods.

questions.q: The Swadeshi movement was sparked by which 1905 event?
questions.explanation: The Swadeshi movement was sparked by the 1905 Partition of Bengal.

questions.q: The Swadeshi movement urged Indians to boycott goods of which origin?
questions.explanation: The movement called for boycotting British goods in favour of indigenous products.

questions.q: Which everyday item was prominently burned in public bonfires during the Swadeshi agitation?
questions.explanation: Foreign cloth was publicly burned as a symbol of rejecting British manufactures.

questions.q: The Swadeshi movement encouraged the growth of which sector within India?
questions.explanation: The movement encouraged native industry, education, and self-reliance.

questions.q: The Swadeshi movement was closely linked with the call for which complementary action?
questions.explanation: Swadeshi and Boycott went together: using one's own goods while boycotting foreign ones.

questions.q: In which Indian province did the Swadeshi movement first take its strongest hold?
questions.explanation: The Swadeshi movement began and was strongest in Bengal, the partitioned province.

questions.q: The Swadeshi movement promoted self-reliance in the spirit of which broader goal?
questions.explanation: Self-reliance through Swadeshi was tied to the larger aspiration for Swaraj, or self-government.

questions.q: The Swadeshi movement encouraged Indians to set up alternatives in which area besides industry?
questions.explanation: The movement promoted national education alongside indigenous industry and goods.

questions.q: Which slogan-song became associated with the Swadeshi movement?
questions.explanation: 'Vande Mataram' became the rallying song of the Swadeshi era.

questions.q: Which leader popularized the Swadeshi idea in western India with the cry that Swaraj was his birthright?
questions.explanation: Tilak vigorously spread Swadeshi and Boycott ideas in Maharashtra and beyond.

questions.q: The trio of Lal-Bal-Pal championed the Swadeshi movement; who was the 'Lal' among them?
questions.explanation: Lala Lajpat Rai, the 'Lal' of Lal-Bal-Pal, was a leading Swadeshi-era nationalist.

questions.q: The 'Bal' of the Lal-Bal-Pal trio associated with Swadeshi refers to whom?
questions.explanation: Bal Gangadhar Tilak was the 'Bal' of the assertive Lal-Bal-Pal nationalist trio.

questions.q: The 'Pal' of Lal-Bal-Pal, a Swadeshi orator from Bengal, was who?
questions.explanation: Bipin Chandra Pal of Bengal completed the Lal-Bal-Pal trio of Swadeshi leaders.

questions.q: The Swadeshi movement led to the founding of which national education body in Bengal in 1906?
questions.explanation: The National Council of Education was founded in Bengal in 1906 to promote nationalist education.

questions.q: Which textile enterprise symbolized Swadeshi industrial effort during this period?
options: Indigenous handloom and mill cloth ventures
questions.explanation: Swadeshi spurred indigenous handloom and Indian-owned mill ventures to replace foreign cloth.

questions.q: The Bengal National College, founded during the Swadeshi era, was associated with which thinker as principal?
questions.explanation: Aurobindo Ghosh served as the first principal of the Bengal National College during the Swadeshi movement.

questions.q: Swadeshi economic thinking was influenced by which earlier theory of colonial exploitation?
questions.explanation: Dadabhai Naoroji's Drain of Wealth theory informed the economic logic of Swadeshi self-reliance.

questions.q: The Swadeshi movement reached a turning point at which 1907 Congress session that split the party?
questions.explanation: The Surat split of 1907 divided Congress Moderates and Extremists, partly over Swadeshi and boycott tactics.

questions.q: Which form of protest, going beyond boycott of goods, was advocated by the extremist wing during Swadeshi?
options: Boycott of British schools, courts, and titles
questions.explanation: Extremists extended Swadeshi into boycotting British schools, courts, titles, and services, anticipating later movements.

questions.q: Swadeshi ideals of hand-spinning and self-reliance were later carried forward most prominently by whom?
questions.explanation: Gandhi revived and deepened the Swadeshi ethic through khadi and the charkha in later decades.

questions.q: The Swadeshi enterprise Bengal Chemical and Pharmaceutical Works was founded by which scientist-nationalist?
questions.explanation: Prafulla Chandra Ray founded Bengal Chemical and Pharmaceutical Works, a flagship Swadeshi industrial venture.

questions.q: In which year was an early form of the Swadeshi idea first publicly resolved at a meeting in the Calcutta Town Hall before the wider 1905 movement?
questions.explanation: A formal Swadeshi resolution was passed at the Calcutta Town Hall meeting on 7 August 1905, often marked as the movement's start.

questions.q: The date 7 August, marking the 1905 Swadeshi pledge, is today commemorated in India as which day?
questions.explanation: 7 August is observed as National Handloom Day in memory of the 1905 launch of the Swadeshi movement.

questions.q: Which moderate leader, while supporting Swadeshi, cautioned against the more aggressive boycott methods of the extremists?
questions.explanation: Gokhale and the Moderates favoured constructive Swadeshi but were wary of the extremists' wider boycott programme.

questions.q: Rabindranath Tagore contributed to the Swadeshi movement notably by composing which patriotic Bengali song collection?
options: Amar Sonar Bangla and related songs
questions.explanation: Tagore composed 'Amar Sonar Bangla' and other songs during the Swadeshi-era anti-partition agitation.

questions.q: The Swadeshi steamship venture launched to challenge British shipping in eastern India was associated with which entrepreneur?
questions.explanation: V. O. Chidambaram Pillai launched the Swadeshi Steam Navigation Company to compete with British shipping lines.

questions.q: Which Swadeshi-era technique of mass protest involved shopkeepers and workers shutting establishments in unison?
questions.explanation: The hartal, a coordinated shutdown of shops and work, was widely used during the Swadeshi agitation.

questions.q: Swadeshi industrial self-reliance later inspired which 1906-founded Indian-owned bank that survived into modern times?
questions.explanation: Several Indian-owned banks, including Bank of India founded in 1906, reflected the Swadeshi drive for indigenous enterprise.

questions.q: The Swadeshi movement's emphasis on 'atmasakti' (self-strength) was articulated most philosophically by which Bengali thinker?
questions.explanation: Aurobindo Ghosh gave the Swadeshi movement a philosophical framing around 'atmasakti', or self-strength and self-reliance.
